artificial intelligence;

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Upon completion of the public comment period described in subsection (a), the Secretary of the Treasury shall conduct research on the innovative or novel methods, techniques, or strategies that regulated financial institutions use, or have the potential to use, to detect illicit activity, such as money laundering, involving digital assets that were identified in such public comment period.

children.label: A
children.text: Improvements in the ability of financial institutions to detect illicit activity involving digital assets.

children.label: B
children.text: Costs to regulated financial institutions.

children.label: C
children.text: The amount and sensitivity of information that is collected or reviewed.

children.label: D
children.text: Privacy risks associated with the information that is collected or reviewed.

children.label: E
children.text: Operational challenges and efficiency considerations.

children.label: F
children.text: Cybersecurity risks.

children.label: G
children.text: Effectiveness of methods, techniques, or strategies at mitigating illicit finance.
the source of illicit activity, such as money laundering and sanctions evasion involving digital assets;

the effectiveness of and gaps in existing methods, techniques, and strategies used by regulated financial institutions in detecting illicit activity, such as money laundering, involving digital assets;
the impact of existing regulatory frameworks on the use and development of innovative methods, techniques, or strategies by regulated financial institutions; and
any foreign jurisdictions that pose a high risk of facilitating illicit activity through the use of digital assets to obtain fiat currency.
The implementation of innovative or novel methods, techniques, or strategies by regulated financial institutions to detect illicit activity involving digital assets.

Standards for payment stablecoin issuers to identify and report illicit activity involving the payment stablecoin of a permitted payment stablecoin issuer, including, fraud, cybercrime, money laundering, financing of terrorism, sanctions evasion, or insider trading.

Standards for payment stablecoin issuers’ systems and practices to monitor transactions on blockchains, digital asset mixing services, tumblers, or other similar services that mix payment stablecoins in such a way as to make such transaction or the identity of the transaction parties less identifiable.
Tailored risk management standards for financial institutions interacting with decentralized finance protocols.

children.heading: Recommendations and report to Congress
children.label: e Recommendations and report to Congress
A
children.text: legislative and regulatory proposals to allow regulated financial institutions to develop and implement novel and innovative methods, techniques, or strategies to detect illicit activity, such as money laundering and sanctions evasion, involving digital assets;

children.label: B
children.text: the results of the research and risk assessments conducted pursuant to this section;

children.label: C
children.text: efforts to support the ability of financial institutions to implement novel and innovative methods, techniques, or strategies to detect illicit activity, such as money laundering and sanctions evasion, involving digital assets;

children.label: D
children.text: the extent to which transactions on distributed ledgers, digital asset mixing services, tumblers, or other similar services that mix payment stablecoins in such a way as to make such transaction or the identity of the transaction parties less identifiable may facilitate illicit activity; and

children.label: E
children.text: legislative recommendations relating to the scope of the term “digital asset service provider” and the application of that term to decentralized finance.

children.heading: Classified annex
children.label: 2 Classified annex
children.text: A report under this section may include a classified annex, if applicable.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: f Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to limit the existing authority of the Secretary of the Treasury or the primary Federal payment stablecoin regulators to, prior to the submission of a report required under this section, use existing exemptive authorities, the no-action letter process, or rulemaking authorities in a manner that encourages regulated financial institutions to adopt novel or innovative methods, techniques, or strategies to detect illicit activity, such as money laundering, involving digital assets.
